messages.content:
Rewrite this Condolence message so that it sounds more natural, as if a person that speak english as their first language wrote it. Try to stay as close as possible to the original wording, that is VERY important:
"Our condolences.

I'm so sorry to hear that she couldn't fight through this. I hope that she did not suffer and that you and Jack had the chance to say your goodbyes. If you need anything, feel free to send me a mail any time you want to. We are all here for you, Mark."

Our deepest condolences.

I'm so sorry to hear that she couldn't overcome this. My hope is that she didn't suffer and that you and Jack had the opportunity to say your goodbyes. If there's anything you need, don't hesitate to reach out to us. We're all here for you, Mark.
If you send this message as a mail to someone, what's the best way to title it?

A good subject line for this type of message might be "Sympathy for your loss" or "Condolences on your recent loss". This will let the recipient know that the message is a condolence message and that it is not spam or something that can be ignored.
How about "Our deepest condolences, Mark". Would that be acceptable and natural sounding?

"Our deepest condolences, Mark" would be an appropriate and natural-sounding subject line for a message expressing condolences to someone named Mark who has recently experienced a loss. It clearly communicates the purpose of the message and allows the recipient to quickly identify the content of the email.

Rewrite this Condolence message so that it sounds more natural, as if a person that speak english as their first language wrote it. Try to stay as close as possible to the original wording, that is VERY important:
"I'm so sorry to hear that Jessy couldn't overcome this. I hope that she didn't suffer and that you and Jack had the opportunity to say your goodbyes. Feel free to tell us if you think there's anything we can do to help you go through this. We are all here for you, Mark."

I'm so sorry to hear about Jessy. My heart goes out to you and Jack, and I hope that she didn't suffer. If there's anything we can do to help you through this, please don't hesitate to reach out. We're all here for you, Mark.

Rewrite this Condolence message so that it sounds more natural, as if a person that speak english as their first language wrote it. Try to stay as close as possible to the original wording, that is VERY important:
"I'm so sorry to hear that Jessy couldn't overcome this. I hope that she didn't suffer and that you and Jack had the opportunity to say your goodbyes. Please tell us if you think there's anything we can do to help you go through this. We are all here for you, Mark."

I'm so sorry to hear about Jessy. My heart goes out to you and Jack during this difficult time. I hope that she didn't suffer and that you were able to say your goodbyes. If there's anything we can do to support you, please don't hesitate to let us know. We're all here for you, Mark.
